result:
I’d give every human brain a built-in “other-mind simulator”: a involuntary, always-on circuit that, whenever you interact with someone, briefly runs a high-fidelity model of what that person is likely thinking, wanting, fearing and intending—then tags your own feelings with that information before you speak or act.

No mind-reading, no loss of privacy: the simulator only activates in direct, real-time encounters, and it doesn’t reveal hidden secrets; it simply makes the emotional and cognitive contours of the other person as vivid to you as your own. The result would be the collapse of casual cruelty, tribal blindness and strategic dishonesty: you’d feel the sting of your own words in the other person’s nervous system before you said them, and you’d sense the innocence or desperation behind an opponent’s anger before you retaliated. Overnight, politics, markets, relationships and justice systems would stop rewarding the people best at de-humanization and start rewarding those best at mutual problem-solving.

One biological upgrade; cascades of cooperation. Everything else—climate, poverty, war, innovation—gets re-routed through brains that can no longer forget, even for a second, that other minds are as real as their own.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
